display: So what should I try first?
/Interview

My mission is grow my own personal knowledge of AI use, get the best results. develop deliverable products for the company I may join. I am Enterprise architect with deep engineering experience as well as development, I am now trying to get more again. technical and hands on. I want to make deliver real value to business usingf AI. not hype
move to goals

Create a powerapp - canvas app which supports Agent register for Copilot studio agents to capture all elements needed to prepare before you develop, stage agent of definition of ready and to support the development and delivery of the agent through dev, test/uat and definition of done so it can be supported to its release to production.
landing a role in this company as a senior member, bringing my skills, experience and enthisiams, so I feel that I am contributing and I get some job satisfaction.
personal goal, get my knowledge organised to help me with being less stressed as I have ADHD.
problems now
AI hype over substance — businesses being sold promise not delivery
organisations don't have the skills to evaluate what they're buying
enterprise architects being used as rubber stamps not thought leaders
write those and move on
strategies now
read, watch videos on youtube, get ebooks, try to retain the knowledge, thought experiments. prototype things, start projects. work with like minded people if i can find them
always
yes always prototype it
build something and show it working, always share the knowledge with likeminded people
challenges now
keeping focus and not getting distracted by new shiny things, ADHD.
keeping organised and retaining what I learn
I have so many ideas, its about organising and putting systems in place that I can keep on top of them, keep them moving, not have to start again etc. less pain when I context shift
narratives now
I am an experienced, deeply technical architect who can take on any challenge and use cost cutting skills to turn an idea from concept to delivery dealing with each step along the way.
A practical, well considered, ever growing, rubber on the road understanding of how to take AI from an idea into something that can add actual value to the individual and also to an organisation
/remote-control
keep going
It's about outcomes not technology

technology is the enabler, but without people, process and policy the opportunity is wasted, people need to understand that AI is not a magic fix, they have to adapt their business and their behavior to get the best out of it
I am always growing and learning, I never stop

build it simply, test it early, iterate fast, strong foundations first though, TDD, specs, roadmaps, strong documentation even at the beginning. clear goals and small slices of value as soon as possible.
wisdom now
Do not rush in, plan, measure twice, cut once. Use people as a sounding board as soon as possible, it helps to get your thoughts together
never assume people understand, read the room: 'If the student does not understand, its the teachers fault'
30 years experience takes 30 years
being percise when it matters, provide just enough at the stage of detail required. but do not cut corners

getting the job and finding my feet in the new role, passing an exam I need to do in the next 3 weeks and leaving my current employeer on a high, make sure they feel the pain of me not being there anymore
not bad, pretty good overall, need more exercise, more relax time and less work.

Help me fix the issues reported by /doctor below.

For each issue: briefly explain what the fix will do, then ask me to confirm before running any shell command that deletes files, modifies global config, or changes my installation. Safe read-only checks are fine without asking. If a suggested fix looks wrong for my setup, say so instead of running it.

- Settings (/home/jellypai/.claude/settings.json › permissions.allow): Invalid permission rule "mcp__*" was skipped: Wildcard tool name "mcp__*" is not supported in allow rules. An allow pattern must name the scope it widens — globs are permitted only in the tool position after a literal mcp__<server>__ prefix. Deny and ask rules accept wildcards anywhere
